pages.name: Help Center Home
pages.description: Entry point for the dropshipping platform Help Center with global search and category navigation.
primary_functions: Let users search all help articles using a prominent global search bar
Provide quick access to main help categories such as Shipping & Fulfillment, Store setup, Store Integrations, Payments & Payouts, Customer Support & Messaging, and Billing & subscriptions
Surface featured or popular articles such as Shipping Policy Template and Carrier comparison
Provide a visible link back to the main platform navigation labeled 'Help Center'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit global search form in the header or hero search bar
Click on a category tile such as 'Shipping & Fulfillment' or 'Store setup'
Click on a featured or popular article card such as 'Shipping Policy Template'

access_methods.description: Accessed via the main platform navigation link labeled 'Help Center Home' (index.html)
Users can land here directly from external links, bookmarks, or onboarding flows

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Displays search results with filters and sorting for help articles.
primary_functions: Show a list of articles matching a search query from the global search bar (e.g., 'shipping policy template', 'SYNC_409 product feed error')
Offer filter chips to narrow results by category such as Store setup, Billing & subscriptions, Shipping & Fulfillment, etc.
Provide a sort dropdown with options like 'Most popular' and 'Most recent' and apply the selected sort to the results
Allow users to open an article by clicking its title to go to the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: Search query entered into the global search bar, e.g., 'shipping policy template' or 'SYNC_409 product feed error'.
Serialized filters applied to the search (e.g., selected category such as 'store_setup').
Sort key for results such as 'most_popular' or 'most_recent'.
Current results page number, starting from 1.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an article title in the search results list

access_methods.description: Opened when a search is performed and the app routes to search_results.html with a query parameter.
Users can bookmark a specific search URL and return to it directly.

pages.name: Category Listing
pages.description: Template page that lists articles within a specific help category.
primary_functions: Display articles for the selected category based on a URL parameter (e.g., categoryId=shipping_fulfillment, store_setup, store_integrations, payments_payouts, customer_support_messaging, billing_subscriptions)
Provide category-specific filters such as integration filters and logos (e.g., a Shopify filter on the Store Integrations category) or content-type filters
Allow users to browse and scroll through category articles such as 'Carrier comparison', 'Payout schedules and minimum thresholds', and 'Return and refund message templates'
Enable navigation to individual articles by clicking article titles to open the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: HelpCategory.id indicating which category to display (e.g., 'shipping_fulfillment', 'store_integrations').
Optional integration platform filter for the Store Integrations category (e.g., 'shopify').
Optional article type filter such as 'guide', 'template', or 'comparison'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an article title in the category listing

access_methods.description: Accessed from header links such as 'Shipping & Fulfillment', 'Store setup', 'Store Integrations', etc., each passing the appropriate categoryId.
Accessed via category tiles on the Help Center Home, which link to category.html with a categoryId parameter.

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Generic article page used for all Help Center content, including guides, templates, comparisons, and troubleshooting docs.
primary_functions: Display full article content including headings, step-by-step instructions, tables, screenshots, and embedded media
Provide a share or link icon near the article title that opens share options including a 'Copy URL to clipboard' action for articles such as the Shipping Policy Template

Support interactive content such as the Carrier comparison table with region dropdown (e.g., United States), weight range filter (02 kg), price filters (Max cost), and sort options (e.g., Fastest delivery time), plus clickable carrier rows that open an on-page carrier detail panel with 'Use this carrier' or 'View setup instructions' buttons

Embed tutorial videos and playlists for integration guides (e.g., Shopify setup) with visible video duration labels and a central Play button to start videos, including the ability to select a video under 10 minutes

Include contextual links and buttons to open other pages such as 'Contact support to change plan' (to Support Request Form), 'Open order automation settings' (to Order Automation Settings), 'Open payout settings' (to Payout Settings), 'Create canned response from this template' (to Canned Response Editor), and 'View system status page' (to System Status Overview)
Present specialized sections like 'Defective item return template' where users can review and use message templates as the basis for canned responses

incoming_params.description: HelpArticle.slug identifying which article to display (e.g., 'shipping-policy-template', 'carrier-comparison').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a contextual link such as 'Contact support to change plan' within the article body
Click the 'Open order automation settings' link in an order automation article section
Click the 'Open payout settings' link in a payouts-related article
Click 'Create canned response from this template' under the 'Defective item return template' section
Click the 'View system status page' link in the SYNC_409 troubleshooting article
Click 'Use this carrier' or 'View setup instructions' in the carrier comparison table after calling startCarrierSetup

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user clicks any article link and the app routes to article.html with a slug parameter.
Articles can be opened directly via their canonical URLs or short share URLs copied from the share menu.

pages.name: Support Request Form
pages.description: Form page for submitting support requests, including billing and subscription changes.
primary_functions: Provide a structured form that can be opened from articles via links like 'Contact support to change plan'
Offer a 'Category' dropdown with options such as 'Billing & subscriptions' to categorize the request
Offer a 'Topic' or 'Issue type' dropdown with options such as 'Downgrade to Basic plan' or similar
Provide a message or description text area where users can type free-form requests such as 'downgrade from Pro to Basic' and specify constraints like 'maximum budget is $30/month'
Capture required account metadata fields like 'Account ID' and 'Store name' with support for placeholder values when needed
Submit the completed support request using a 'Submit' or 'Preview request' button

incoming_params.description: HelpArticle.id of the article that linked to the support form, used to preconfigure category/topic and to track context (e.g., plan change guide).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Help Center' or similar link after submitting or previewing the support request

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact Support' footer link, which opens a blank support request form.
Opened from contextual article links (such as plan change articles) that pass a sourceArticleId parameter.

pages.name: Order Automation Settings
pages.description: Settings page for configuring order automation rules such as auto-canceling unfulfilled orders.
primary_functions: Display the current order automation configuration with a clear section for auto-canceling unfulfilled orders
Provide a toggle or checkbox labeled 'Enable auto-cancel for unfulfilled orders' to turn the rule on or off
Allow users to set the timeframe for cancellation via a numeric field or dropdown labeled 'Cancel after' or 'Unfulfilled for' with options including '7 days'
Offer a 'Save changes' or 'Update automation' button that applies the selected auto-cancellation rules

incoming_params.description: Optional HelpArticle.id indicating the help article that linked to these settings (e.g., the auto-cancel unfulfilled orders article).

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user clicks 'Open order automation settings' from a help article or navigates here from the main platform settings.

pages.name: Payout Settings
pages.description: Settings page for configuring payout schedules and minimum payout thresholds.
primary_functions: Show current payout configuration including frequency and minimum payout amount
Provide a 'Payout frequency' dropdown with options such as 'Daily', 'Weekly', and 'Monthly', allowing users to select 'Weekly'
Allow users to set a minimum payout amount using an input field labeled 'Minimum payout amount' or 'Threshold (USD)' and enter a value such as '100'
Include a currency selector next to the threshold field to confirm or change currency (e.g., set to 'USD')
Offer a 'Save payout settings' or 'Update schedule' button that applies the selected weekly schedule and minimum threshold

incoming_params.description: Optional HelpArticle.id of the payouts-related article that linked to this settings page.

access_methods.description: Opened when a user clicks 'Open payout settings' from a payouts help article or from the main platform payout configuration.

pages.name: Canned Response Editor
pages.description: Editor page for creating and saving canned responses based on message templates from articles.
primary_functions: Load template text from a source article section such as 'Defective item return template' when opened via a 'Create canned response from this template' button
Allow users to name the canned response by entering text into a 'Title' or 'Name' field (e.g., 'Defective item return - template A')
Provide a rich text editor showing the template body so users can review and modify content, including replacing placeholders like '[DISCOUNT]' with specific values such as '10% discount'
Offer a 'Category' or 'Folder' dropdown where users can assign the response to a grouping such as 'Returns & refunds'
Save the new canned response using a 'Save canned response' or 'Create template' button for later use in customer support workflows

incoming_params.description: ArticleTemplateSnippet.id representing the specific template snippet used to seed the canned response.
HelpArticle.id of the article that provided the template snippet.

access_methods.description: Opened from a help article's 'Create canned response from this template' button, which passes the templateSnippetId (and optionally sourceArticleId).

pages.name: System Status Overview
pages.description: Status dashboard for viewing the health and incident history of platform services.
primary_functions: Provide a summary view of overall platform health with service-level indicators for areas like 'Product Sync' or 'Catalog sync'
Offer filter controls or checkboxes to focus on specific services such as 'Product Sync'
List recent and ongoing incidents or events for the selected services with timestamps and short descriptions
Allow users to click an incident entry to open the Incident Detail page for more information and reporting
Serve as the target for links like 'View system status page' coming from troubleshooting articles such as those describing SYNC_409 errors

incoming_params.description: Optional service slug (e.g., 'product_sync') used to focus the overview on a particular service.
Optional HelpArticle.id indicating that the user navigated here from a troubleshooting article.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a specific incident in the incidents list

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'System status' link in the header navigation.
Accessible via the 'System status' footer link.
Opened with optional serviceSlug when navigated from a troubleshooting article that links directly to system status.

pages.name: Incident Detail & Report Issue
pages.description: Detail view for a specific incident with controls to report related issues.
primary_functions: Display detailed information about a specific incident including title, impact, affected services, and timeline updates
Provide a prominent 'Report an issue' or 'Report incident' button that reveals or scrolls to an incident report form
Offer an incident report form with a subject field for entries such as 'SYNC_409 product feed not updating'
Include a severity dropdown with options like Low, Medium, High, allowing selection of 'Medium'
Provide a description text area where users can describe issues, including text like 'SYNC_409 error for product feed updates'
Submit the incident report using a 'Submit report' button so the issue is logged against the incident

incoming_params.description: StatusIncident.id that identifies which incident's details and report form to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a breadcrumb or 'Back to system status' link from the incident detail view

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user selects an incident from the System Status Overview, passing incidentId in the URL.

pages.name: About Help Center
pages.description: Informational page describing the Help Center and how to use it effectively.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose and scope of the Help Center for the ecommerce dropshipping platform
Describe how to use key features such as the global search bar, category browsing, and status pages
Provide links to important sections and categories to help new users get started quickly
Offer guidance on when to use self-service articles versus contacting support via the Support Request Form

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a highlighted link or button that routes users to the Help Center Home
Click a highlighted link that routes users directly to the Contact Support form

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About Help Center' footer link on all pages.

pages.name: Terms of Use
pages.description: Legal terms and conditions for using the Help Center and related content.
primary_functions: Present the legal terms under which users may access and use Help Center content and tools
Describe limitations of liability, acceptable use, and other conditions relevant to help documentation
Provide a stable reference link for legal terms from the footer across the site

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Terms of use' footer link.

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Page explaining how user data is collected, stored, and used in the context of the Help Center.
primary_functions: Describe what data is collected when users browse the Help Center, submit support requests, or report incidents
Explain how collected data is stored, protected, and potentially shared
Provide information about user rights and options related to privacy and data usage
Serve as a reference link for privacy information from the footer across the site

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Privacy policy' footer link.

header_links.description: Help Center homepage with search and category tiles
Articles on shipping methods, carriers, and fulfillment workflows
Guides and templates for configuring your store settings and policies
Help for integrating with platforms like Shopify and other sales channels
Documentation on payments, payout schedules, and minimum thresholds
Customer support, messaging workflows, and canned response templates
Information about subscription plans, billing, and plan changes
Live system health and product synchronization status

footer_links.description: Learn how to use the Help Center effectively
Open the support request form to get help from the team
Check current platform status and incidents
Read about how your data is collected and used
View the legal terms for using the Help Center
